name: DAT Freight & Analytics API
what_it_does: DAT Freight & Analytics REST API for the largest North American freight marketplace and rate benchmarking platform. Enables AI agents to manage freight load posting and carrier search for spot market truckload procurement, handle real-time and historical spot rate data retrieval for freight cost benchmarking, access lane rate analytics and market intelligence for transportation strategy, retrieve carrier capacity data and truck availability for load coverage, handle freight market capacity indicators (Van, Reefer, Flatbed) for supply/demand analytics, access fuel surcharge and accessorial rate data for total transportation cost calculation, retrieve historical rate trends for transportation budget forecasting, manage load tracking and shipment status for freight visibility, access carrier performance and compliance data for carrier qualification, and integrate freight market data with TMS, procurement, and supply chain analytics platforms.
use_cases: Benchmarking freight rates from TMS procurement agents
Finding carrier capacity from spot freight sourcing agents
Forecasting transportation costs from supply chain planning agents
Monitoring market rates from freight analytics agents
not_for: Parcel shipping without truckload freight market context
International ocean freight without North American trucking focus
Simple rate shopping without freight market analytics needs
best_when: A shipper, 3PL, freight broker, or TMS vendor using DAT wants AI agents to automate spot market rate benchmarking, carrier capacity search, freight market analytics, load posting, and transportation cost forecasting for North American truckload.
avoid_when: ACCURACY RISK: DAT spot rates are market averages — automated load tendering at DAT benchmark prices without carrier negotiation creates procurement errors in volatile markets. DAT historical rates reflect market averages, not specific carrier contract rates; automated contract benchmarking must account for carrier-specific factors. Carrier capacity data is directional, not guaranteed commitment; automated load coverage assumptions without carrier confirmation create service failures.

auth.notes: DAT uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. Developer portal at developer.dat.com. Beaverton, Oregon HQ. Part of Roper Technologies portfolio. 500M+ loads posted annually. Largest North American freight marketplace. DAT One platform combines load board, rate analytics, and TMS integration. Webhooks for load status and market events. REST API with JSON. Primary competitor: Truckstop.com for spot freight load boards; Transplace, Echo for TMS.

pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly/annual subscription; rate analytics API priced separately from load board access
pricing.notes: Beaverton, Oregon. Founded 1978. Part of Roper Technologies. Largest North American freight load board — 500M+ loads/year, 1.7M+ registered trucks. DAT One platform. Rate analytics: iQ analytics, RateView rate benchmarking. Key products: Freight TMS integrations, Power Broker (broker TMS). Competes with Truckstop.com for load board and Transplace/Echo for freight analytics.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; subscription tier dependent

known_agent_gotchas: Rate volatility — freight spot rates are highly volatile (can change 20-40% in weeks); automated rate benchmarking must account for market timing and use recent data windows
Spot vs. contract rates — DAT rates are spot market; contract lane rates negotiate separately; don't use DAT spot benchmark as proxy for carrier contract rate negotiation
Load coverage is not guaranteed — carrier capacity search shows availability indicators, not firm commitments; automated load assignments without carrier acceptance create tender failures
Regional rate variation — freight rates vary significantly by corridor and region; lane-level rate data is more reliable than broad market averages for procurement decisions
Equipment type specificity — Van, Reefer, and Flatbed rate markets are separate; validate equipment type matching for accurate rate benchmarking
Carrier compliance verification — DAT carrier data includes DOT compliance status; automated carrier selection must verify active operating authority and insurance independently
agent_readiness.security_notes: Freight marketplace and analytics. SOC2. OAuth2. US/CA. Freight rate, carrier capacity, and load data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Freight marketplace. SOC2. OAuth2. US/CA. Freight rate and carrier data.
